intent: In the Report Builder mapping modal, let numeric scalar params (int/float/bool) bind to a column the same way str already can (text-or-column toggle), and surface the already-built per-function replace-vs-append output mode in the UI.
definition_of_done: Capability 1: in the Builder mapping modal, int/float/bool params show the same Plain string / Column-backed toggle as str. Text mode broadcasts the literal value (persisted via source_scalar_map) or, when blank, falls back to the Python default; column mode binds one-or-more columns and runs per-record via the existing scalar-run path, with multiple columns forming positional argument bundles (equal-length-among-varying enforced, frontend guard + backend). A param with no value, no binding, AND no Python default is blocked at attach via a generalized _find_missing_bindings (satisfied by binding OR literal OR has_default), with a mirrored frontend Save-guard. Binding eligibility derives in ONE place — binding_kind(type_str) in classification.py off the existing function_class — and suggest._SCALAR_TYPES/_SUGGEST_TYPES + attach._REQUIRES_BINDING are deleted in favor of it; the frontend reads the emitted binding_kind. A numeric param's saved binding (mode, bound columns, order) round-trips on edit-load (Principle 7), reusing the str pattern; switching text->column clears the stale scalar. Runner/bundles/param_resolve/executors are UNCHANGED. Capability 2: each function within a set has its own append-vs-replace control (append_name / ordered replace targets) in the Builder UI; edits go through an extended patch_pipeline_step (a function_output map keyed by function_id updating function_output_config + output_target_map transactionally, no longer writing the vestigial set-level output_mode), via the widened existing step PATCH — no new route, no new table. Every changed or new behavioral guarantee has a test (rule 10).
mvp_scope: Capability 1: int/float/bool params get the 'Plain string / Column-backed' toggle; text mode broadcasts a scalar value (unchanged) or falls back to the Python default; column mode binds one-or-more columns and runs per-record via the existing scalar-run path (multi-column = positional argument bundles, equal-length-among-varying enforced). Type-eligibility is consolidated to a single source (classification descriptor table + a backend-emitted eligibility flag the frontend reads). Capability 2: each function within a set gets its own append-vs-replace control (with append_name / ordered replace targets) in the Builder UI, backed by the existing per-function function_output_config / output_target_map (#264).
explicitly_out_of_scope: The rename built-in step (#40, runs last) — kept exactly as-is, not modified, not removed, not folded into output-mode
Any runner/executor/bundle/param_resolve execution-engine code changes — the runner is already type-agnostic and per-function-output-aware; capability 1 must add no type branches downward
v2 per-source scalar argument persistence beyond what source_scalar_map already does (project-wide deferred, inherited from runner-execution)
pd.DataFrame column-subset binding (rejected in runner-execution; full table always passed)
affected_components: backend/domain/functions/classification.py — _TYPE_DESCRIPTORS / derive_function_class (DB-free leaf): the single source the eligibility flag derives from
backend/domain/functions/suggest.py — suggest_bindings, _SCALAR_TYPES/_SUGGEST_TYPES, param_kind: numerics become column-eligible; sets derived from classification, not parallel literals
backend/domain/functions/attach.py — _REQUIRES_BINDING, _find_missing_bindings, _write_alias_rows: numerics may write alias_map rows; optional-binding rule generalized
backend/domain/functions/step_edit.py — patch_pipeline_step: add a per-function output-config edit path (today updates all members together)
backend api/pipelines.py route — per-function output-mode contract (new granular endpoint vs extended PATCH) — thin, no raw SQL in seam
frontend/screen-builder.jsx — SINGLE-OWNER shared surface: the ~8 str-only conditionals in the mapping modal + the Output selector relocation from set-level to per-function
tests: tests/test_attach.py (numeric=scalar-only assertions), screen-builder.test.jsx (str-only toggle tests)
dependencies: runner-execution (frozen) — adopts its multi_select_eligible definition, multi-param positional-zip binding, and output-target-map / append-replace semantics
#264 — per-function function_output_config + output_target_map already built (capability 2 backend complete)
assumptions: screen-builder.jsx is a single-owner shared surface: both capabilities' UI changes land via one UI work-unit to avoid conflicts; non-UI backend work runs in parallel (slicing is a to-slices concern, recorded here only as a shared-surface note)
The runner requires ZERO code changes for capability 1 (verified type-agnostic in scoping); to be re-verified against current code at build time
parameter.has_default / default_value (#258) hold the Python default a numeric param can fall back to

No regression for existing attachments: a function already attached with a numeric scalar value keeps working — function_class is unchanged by this feature, and on edit-load such a param has current_scalar_value + no current_bindings, so it opens in text mode with its value. No schema migration is needed (capability 1 adds no DDL; numerics can structurally hold alias_map rows already).

resolved_decisions.question: One combined feature or split into two?
resolved_decisions.resolution: Combined. The coupling is the shared UI surface (screen-builder.jsx). User's parallel-build plan (two non-UI agents + one UI agent owning screen-builder.jsx end-to-end) is the single-feature shape. Split was recommended (different blast radii; cap 2 is small) but consciously rejected — shipped/coordinated together.

resolved_decisions.question: Is the rename built-in removed or changed?
resolved_decisions.resolution: Neither. Out-of-scope means untouched: the rename built-in (#40) stays a first-class built-in step, runs last, no edits to builtins.py. Distinct from append/replace output modes.

resolved_decisions.question: Capability 1 — optional binding semantics
resolved_decisions.resolution: Numerics mirror str: binding is OPTIONAL. Text mode with a value broadcasts it (persisted via source_scalar_map, as today); no value falls back to the Python default. Column mode binds one-or-more columns and runs per-record. (Adopted from runner-execution scalar-run model.)

resolved_decisions.question: Capability 1 — multi-column for numerics?
resolved_decisions.resolution: Yes. A column-bound numeric is multi_select_eligible exactly like column_backed/pd.Series: multiple columns form positional argument bundles, equal-length-among-varying enforced. Adopted from runner-execution ('multi-PARAM column binding in scope', zip semantics).

resolved_decisions.question: Capability 1 — classification mechanism (granularity bump?)
resolved_decisions.resolution: NO granularity bump. Investigation showed granularity has no consumer outside classification.py (only derive_function_class, proven invariant under a 0->1 bump) and multi_select_eligible is NOT derived from it — it is a conceptual label; the runner expands any param that has alias_map rows. So bumping int/float/bool to 1 is functionally inert and would muddy the descriptor's documented §11 ordering. Mechanism that actually gates binding = suggest offering a picker + attach writing alias_map rows. Superseded by the binding_kind derivation below.

resolved_decisions.question: OCP — where does binding-eligibility live? (option B, then sharpened)
resolved_decisions.resolution: Single source, NO new field. The three binding behaviors map 1:1 onto the EXISTING function_class: scalar (int/float/bool/str) -> value_or_column (toggle); pd.Series/pd.Series[bool] -> column_only (required); pd.dataframe -> table (never bound). Add ONE shared pure derivation binding_kind(type_str) in classification.py (DB-free leaf, the #51 single source) reading off function_class. Consumers call it and DELETE their literals: suggest._SCALAR_TYPES/_SUGGEST_TYPES, attach._REQUIRES_BINDING; the frontend reads the emitted binding_kind. The 4 duplicated literals existed only because int/float/bool were special-cased away from str; this feature removes that special case, collapsing the rule to 'scalar-class = value_or_column' with nothing left to duplicate. is_validation_return stays orthogonal (classifies the return, not the param).

resolved_decisions.question: Capability 2 — per-function output mode scope
resolved_decisions.resolution: Replace-vs-append at the function level. Backend per-function storage/execution already exists (#264, function_output_config + output_target_map + FunctionSetExecutor). Work is frontend (relocate the Output selector from set-level into each function's group) + a per-function edit path. Confirmed replace-vs-append; rename built-in excluded.

resolved_decisions.question: Optional-default fallback edge: numeric param with no value, no binding, no Python default (state d)
resolved_decisions.resolution: Block at attach. Generalize attach._find_missing_bindings so a param is satisfied by ANY of {a column binding, a literal value, a Python default (has_default)} — otherwise flagged missing-required, exactly like an unbound str/pd.Series today. The frontend Save-guard mirrors the backend reject. Rationale: same 'must give me something to call with' contract that governs str; silently calling the fn with a missing kwarg would crash the worker per-row. has_default/default_value (#258) make the exemption cheap.

resolved_decisions.question: Per-function output-mode API contract: extend step PATCH or new endpoint?
resolved_decisions.resolution: Extend the existing step PATCH; step_edit is the only caller. patch_pipeline_step gains an optional function_output map (function_id -> {output_mode, append_name, output_targets}) that updates function_output_config + output_target_map keyed by (sfm_id, function_id) transactionally, and stops writing the now-vestigial set-level source_function_map.output_mode (it can't represent a mixed set; the runner already reads function_output_config first). pipelines.py widens the existing PATCH body — no new route, no new backend entry point. No new tables (function_output_config + output_target_map exist from #264); any DDL stays in the data/base/schema layer per convention.

resolved_decisions.question: Round-trip / Principle-7 restore for a numeric param's saved binding
resolved_decisions.resolution: Reuses the str pattern, no new mechanics. suggest already returns current_bindings in alias_map.position order (#191/#260) for column-eligible params; once numerics are binding_kind=value_or_column they flow through the same path. Edit-load opens a numeric param in column mode when current_bindings is non-empty (preserving bound-column order), else text mode with current_scalar_value; switching text->column clears the stale scalar (handleSave). Guarded by a round-trip test (open -> save-untouched -> value/mode/order unchanged).
